IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.

If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill using the Skill tool BEFORE any response, any action, any clarifying question.

User instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" does not mean skip workflows. Check for skills FIRST.

## Priority Chain

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md contradicts a skill, follow CLAUDE.md. The user is in control.

## Anti-Rationalization

These thoughts mean STOP — you are rationalizing skipping a skill check:

| Thought                              | Reality                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| "This is simple enough"              | Simple tasks have skills too. Check.                                           |
| "I already know how"                 | Skills contain project-specific overrides you don't have memorized. Invoke it. |
| "Let me explore first"               | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first.                                   |
| "I need more context"                | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. Always.                         |
| "The skill is overkill"              | Simple things become complex. Use it.                                          |
| "I'll just do this one thing first"  | Check BEFORE doing anything. No exceptions.                                    |
| "I remember this skill"              | Skills evolve. Read the current version via the Skill tool.                    |
| "This doesn't need a formal process" | If a skill exists for it, use it. Period.                                      |
| "Let me look at the code quickly"    | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills.                             |
| "This feels productive"              | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this.                         |
| "This is just a discussion"          | Questions about features, bugs, or architecture ARE development tasks. Check.  |

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, commit format, verification): Follow exactly. Do not adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (React patterns, styling, architecture): Adapt principles to context. The spirit matters more than the letter.

The skill itself tells you which type it is. When unclear, treat as rigid.

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills apply:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorm, start, planning, debugging) — these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Domain skills second** (react, typescript, tanstack) — these guide execution
3. **Workflow skills third** (commit, verification, close) — these govern shipping

## Subagent Exemption

If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific, scoped task — skip this enforcement. Subagents follow their prompt, not the full skill ceremony.

# pi-speak — Project Context

## What This Is

A **Pi coding agent extension** that adds voice readback via Unreal Speech TTS. Not a standalone app — runs inside Pi.

## Pi Extension Structure

Read more about Pi Packages and Extensions here:

- https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/packages.md
- https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/extensions.md

## Linting

- `.pi/extensions/checks.ts` auto-runs ESLint + Prettier after every write/edit
- Errors are injected into the conversation automatically
- No manual lint step needed — trust the hook

## Platform

macOS for now. Platform abstraction exists (`src/platform.ts`) for future Linux/Windows support.

## Packaging

Planned as a **pi-package** for distribution via `pi install npm:pi-speak`.

See **README.md** for full details.

# pi-speak

Voice readback extension for [Pi](https://github.com/mariozechner/pi-coding-agent).

## Features

- **Voice readback** — Hear agent responses via Unreal Speech TTS
- **Voice ping** — Spoken summary when agent finishes (LLM-powered via OpenRouter, or fallback)
- **Audio caching** — Responses cached for instant replay
- **Replay shortcut** — `alt+r` to replay last response or stop playback
- **Status widget** — Shows current playback state in the UI
- **Lint feedback** — Auto-runs ESLint + Prettier after write/edit operations
- **macOS support** — Platform abstraction exists for future Linux/Windows support

## Installation

> [!NOTE]
> Not published on npm yet

1. Install the extension:

```bash
pi install git:github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak
```

2. Add your API key to your environment by sourcing `~/.env` in your shell:

```sh
# Add to your shell profile (~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, etc.)
source ~/.env

# Or set it inline:
UNREAL_SPEECH_API_KEY=your-key-here pi
```

3. Reload Pi — the extension is auto-discovered.

## Configuration

On first run, a default config is created at `~/.config/pi-speak/config.json`:

See the default config [here](./src/config/schema.json).

| Key                           | Type         | Default                   | Description                                                 |
| ----------------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `tts.voiceId`                 | string       | `"Sierra"`                | [Unreal Speech voice ID](https://docs.v8.unrealspeech.com/) |
| `tts.bitrate`                 | string       | `"192k"`                  | Audio bitrate                                               |
| `tts.speed`                   | number       | `0`                       | Speech speed (-1.0 to 1.0)                                  |
| `tts.pitch`                   | number       | `1.0`                     | Speech pitch (0.5 to 1.5)                                   |
| `tts.maxChunkChars`           | number       | `900`                     | Max chars per TTS request (API limit: 1000)                 |
| `behavior.shortcut`           | string       | `"alt+r"`                 | Keyboard shortcut for replay/stop                           |
| `behavior.pingEnabled`        | boolean      | `true`                    | Speak ping on agent_end                                     |
| `behavior.pingOnStartEnabled` | boolean      | `false`                   | Speak welcome on session_start                              |
| `behavior.fallbackPingText`   | string       | `"Work finished."`        | Fallback ping text when summarizer disabled                 |
| `summarizer.enabled`          | boolean      | `true`                    | Use LLM for ping summaries (vs fallback)                    |
| `summarizer.model`            | string       | `"openai/gpt-oss-20b"`    | [OpenRouter model](https://openrouter.ai/models)            |
| `summarizer.maxTokens`        | number       | `60`                      | Max tokens for summarizer                                   |
| `summarizer.timeoutMs`        | number       | `5000`                    | Timeout for summarizer API call                             |
| `debug.enabled`               | boolean      | `true`                    | Write to debug log                                          |
| `debug.logPath`               | string       | `"~/.pi-speak-debug.log"` | Path to debug log file                                      |
| `debug.logMaxBytes`           | number       | `2097152`                 | Max size before log rotation                                |
| `api.unrealSpeechKey`         | string\|null | `null`                    | Unreal Speech API key (env var takes precedence)            |
| `api.openRouterKey`           | string\|null | `null`                    | OpenRouter API key (env var takes precedence)               |

## Usage

- **Notification**: When the agent finishes, a notification appears with the session name and summary. Navigate to the session yourself, then press `alt+r` to hear the response.
- **`alt+r`**: Press to replay the last response. Press again during playback to stop.

## Debugging

Set `PI_SPEAK_DEBUG=0` to disable the debug log, or set `"debug": false` in config.
Log file: `~/.pi-speak-debug.log`

## Releases

This project uses [release-please](https://github.com/googleapis/release-please) for automated versioning and changelog generation via [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).

## TODO

- [x] Add release-please
- [x] Config location follows XDG spec (`~/.config/pi-speak/config.json`) — no standard convention exists for pi-package extension config
- [x] Config Migration Support
- [x] Remove ~/.env file loading (use process.env directly)
- [x] If muted, don't generate TTS to save costs.
  - don't play the summarizer, and show notification for the readback inside the TUI that its muted
- [ ] Publish on npm
- [ ] Linux support
- [ ] Windows support

# Code Review: pi-speak

## Context

Reviewing the codebase for faults, sloppy code, and general improvements. This is a Pi coding agent extension for voice readback via Unreal Speech TTS.

---

## Issues Found

### 🔴 Critical Issues

1. **Non-macOS platforms silently do nothing**
   - **File**: `src/platform.ts`
   - **Problem**: `createPlatform()` returns a no-op implementation for non-darwin platforms. The user gets no feedback that their OS isn't supported.
   - **Impact**: Extension appears broken on Linux/Windows with no explanation.

2. **API calls have no timeout**
   - **Files**: `src/tts.ts`, `src/summarizer.ts`
   - **Problem**: `fetchTTS()` and `summarizeForPing()` use `fetch()` without a `signal` abort controller or timeout option.
   - **Impact**: Network issues can hang the extension indefinitely.

3. **`.env` values can shadow real environment variables**
   - **File**: `src/helpers.ts` → `loadEnvKey()`
   - **Problem**: Always reads from `~/.env` even if the key already exists in `process.env`.
   - **Impact**: If a user sets `UNREAL_SPEECH_API_KEY` in their shell and ALSO has it in `~/.env`, the `~/.env` value wins. This is confusing because `process.env` should take precedence.

---

### 🟡 Medium Issues

4. **Unused function: `shellQuote`**
   - **File**: `src/helpers.ts`
   - **Problem**: Function is defined but never imported or used.
   - **Impact**: Dead code that could confuse future maintainers.

5. **`config.debug` field is unused**
   - **File**: `src/config.ts`, `src/debug.ts`
   - **Problem**: The `SpeakConfig.debug` field exists and is documented, but `debug.ts` only checks `process.env.PI_SPEAK_DEBUG`. The config value is never read.
   - **Impact**: Config option doesn't work; users are confused why setting `"debug": false` doesn't disable logging.

6. **Debug log rotation silently fails**
   - **File**: `src/debug.ts` → `maybeRotate()`
x  - **Problem**: If the log file can't be accessed (permissions, etc.), the error is swallowed with no feedback.
   - **Impact**: Hard to diagnose why debug logging isn't working.

7. **Cache clearing swallows errors**
   - **File**: `src/tts.ts` → `clearCache()`
   - **Problem**: `unlinkSync` failures are silently ignored.
   - **Impact**: Partial cache cleanup failures go unnoticed.

8. **`stop()` uses SIGTERM, not guaranteed to work**
   - **File**: `src/tts.ts`
   - **Problem**: `currentPlayback.kill("SIGTERM")` — the `afplay` process may not respond to SIGTERM gracefully.
   - **Impact**: Playback might not stop cleanly.

---

### 🟢 Minor Issues / Style

9. **Config imports are at the bottom of `src/config.ts`**
   - The imports appear after the JSDoc for the main extension. While this works, it's unconventional. All imports should be at the very top of the file for readability.

10. **ESLint `strict` rules may fail on some files**
    - **File**: `eslint.config.ts`
    - **Problem**: Using `...tseslint.configs.strict.rules` with `moduleResolution: "bundler"` in tsconfig might cause issues with module resolution. The `typeRoots` configuration also points to a specific machine path.
    - **Impact**: ESLint might not run correctly in other environments.

11. **Inconsistent error handling patterns**
    - Some places use `err: unknown`, others catch and ignore silently.
    - Consider standardizing: always log to debug, always notify user for recoverable errors.

12. **Type annotations on function parameters could be cleaner**
    - **File**: `src/tts.ts` → `speak()`
    - The `ui` parameter uses inline type literal `{ setWidget: ...; notify: ... }` instead of a shared interface. This appears in multiple places.

13. **`newFiles` array can grow unnecessarily**
    - **File**: `src/tts.ts` → `speak()`
    - If playback is aborted mid-chunk, `newFiles` still contains paths that were fetched but never cached. The old cache cleanup in `finally` only removes files NOT in `newFiles`, so orphaned files may accumulate.

14. **Race condition in cache management**
    - **File**: `src/tts.ts` → `speak()`
    - If the same response is triggered twice rapidly, `clearCache()` runs in the caller before `speak()` starts. But if `speak()` is called multiple times concurrently, both could try to clear cache and write to `cachedAudioFiles` simultaneously.

---

## Reusable Utilities Found

- `chunkBySentences(text, maxChars)` — `src/helpers.ts` ✓ well-structured
- `stripMarkdown(text)` — `src/helpers.ts` ✓ comprehensive markdown stripping
- `loadEnvKey(key)` — `src/helpers.ts` ✓ works, but see issue #3

---

## Recommendations

| Priority | Action |
|----------|--------|
| High | Add platform detection with user-facing warning for unsupported OS |
| High | Add `AbortSignal.timeout()` to all `fetch()` calls |
| High | Fix `loadEnvKey()` to check `process.env` first |
| Medium | Remove `shellQuote` or use it |
| Medium | Wire `config.debug` into `debug.ts` |
| Medium | Add proper error logging in `maybeRotate()` and `clearCache()` |
| Low | Clean up import ordering |
| Low | Share UI interface types instead of inline literals |
| Low | Consider SIGKILL fallback in `stop()` |

---

## Verification

To verify fixes:
1. Run `npm run lint:check` — ensure no ESLint errors
2. Run `npm run format:check` — ensure consistent formatting
3. Test on non-macOS platform — expect a clear error message
4. Test with network disabled — expect timeout error, not hang
5. Test with `process.env` set AND `~/.env` set — expect `process.env` value to win

content.text:
name: release-please
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  release-please:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      releases_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs.releases_created }}
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        id: release
        with:
          config-file: .github/release-please-config.json
          manifest-file: .github/.release-please-manifest.json

  publish:
    needs: release-please
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only publish when a new release was created
    if: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.releases_created == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Publish to npm
        run: npm publish --access public
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

# Prepare First npm Release — pi-speak

## Context

Preparing the first npm release (`@nbr/pi-speak`) for the pi-speak voice readback extension. The codebase is functional but lacks:
- **Unit tests** (no test framework)
- **CI/CD quality gates** (lint/type-check not in CI)
- **Complete documentation** for contributors
- **Code quality improvements** from previous review

---

## Approach

1. **Add test framework** (Vitest) with unit tests for pure functions
2. **Set up CI quality gates** — run lint, type-check, and tests on PR
3. **Address code review issues** — review the 14 issues found previously
4. **Improve documentation** — add CONTRIBUTING.md, improve README
5. **Verify release setup** — ensure npm metadata is complete

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `package.json` | Add Vitest, add test scripts, update npm metadata |
| `vitest.config.ts` | New — Vitest configuration |
| `src/**/*.test.ts` | New — Unit tests for pure functions |
| `src/**/*.test.ts` | New — Config validation tests |
| `src/**/*.test.ts` | New — Mocked API tests for TTS/summarizer |
| `.github/workflows/ci.yml` | New — CI workflow with lint/type/test |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | New — Contribution guidelines |
| `README.md` | Update — Add badges, improve sections |
| `src/helpers.ts` | Fix issues from code review |
| `src/debug.ts` | Fix issues from code review |
| `src/tts.ts` | Fix issues from code review |

---

## Reuse

### Existing Functions Ready for Testing

| Function | Location | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| `stripMarkdown(text)` | `src/helpers.ts:12` | Markdown → plain text |
| `chunkBySentences(text, maxChars)` | `src/helpers.ts:46` | Split text into chunks |
| `summarize(text, maxLen)` | `src/helpers.ts:85` | One-line summary |
| `loadConfig()` | `src/config/index.ts:23` | Load + validate config |
| `migrate(raw, fromVersion)` | `src/config/migrations.ts:43` | Config version migration |
| `SpeakConfigSchema.safeParse()` | `src/config/schema.ts` | Zod validation |

### Existing Test Infrastructure

| Script | Location | Status |
|--------|----------|--------|
| `npm run test:lint` | ESLint | ✅ Working |
| `npm run test:format` | Prettier | ✅ Working |
| `npm run test:compile` | TypeScript | ✅ Working |
| `npm run test:config` | Config validation | ✅ Working |

---

## Issues from Initial Code Review

Check which of the 14 issues are still relevant:

| # | Issue | Status | Fix |
|---|-------|--------|-----|
| 1 | Non-macOS platforms silently do nothing | ⚠️ Partial | Add proper warning in `session_start` (already has warning?) |
| 2 | API calls have no timeout | ✅ Fixed | `AbortSignal.timeout()` already used |
| 3 | `.env` values shadow env vars | ⚠️ Unclear | Check if still an issue |
| 4 | Unused function `shellQuote` | ✅ Likely Fixed | Should verify removed |
| 5 | `config.debug` unused | ⚠️ Unclear | Check debug.ts |
| 6 | Debug log rotation silently fails | ⚠️ Fix | Add proper error logging |
| 7 | Cache clearing swallows errors | ⚠️ Fix | Add debug logging |
| 8 | `stop()` uses SIGTERM | ⚠️ Already fixed | Has SIGKILL fallback |
| 9 | Config imports at bottom | ⚠️ Style | Fix import ordering |
| 10 | ESLint path issues | ⚠️ Fix | Use relative paths |
| 11 | Inconsistent error handling | ⚠️ Improve | Standardize patterns |
| 12 | UI type annotations inline | ⚠️ Low priority | Create shared interface |
| 13 | `newFiles` can grow unnecessarily | ⚠️ Low priority | Cleanup orphaned files |
| 14 | Race condition in cache | ⚠️ Low priority | Add locking |

---

## Steps

- [ ] **Step 1: Add Vitest to project**
  - Install `vitest`, `@vitest/coverage-v8`, `@vitest/ui`
  - Add `vitest.config.ts` with TypeScript support
  - Update `package.json` with test scripts

- [ ] **Step 2: Write unit tests for helpers**
  - `src/helpers.test.ts`:
    - `stripMarkdown()` — code blocks, inline code, bold, italic, links, headers
    - `chunkBySentences()` — normal splits, edge cases (no punctuation, very long)
    - `summarize()` — short text, long text, unicode

- [ ] **Step 3: Write config tests**
  - `src/config/index.test.ts`:
    - Valid configs parse correctly
    - Invalid values fail validation
    - v0 → v1 migration preserves values
  - `src/config/migrations.test.ts`:
    - v0 flat config migrates to v1 nested

- [ ] **Step 4: Add mocked API tests**
  - `src/tts.test.ts`:
    - Test `chunkBySentences` integration
    - Mock `fetchTTS` to test error handling
    - Test generation cancellation logic
  - `src/summarizer.test.ts`:
    - Test fallback when summarizer disabled
    - Test fallback when no API key
    - Mock OpenRouter to test LLM summary

- [ ] **Step 5: Set up CI workflow**
  - Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
  - Run lint, type-check, and tests on push/PR
  - Add test coverage reporting

- [ ] **Step 6: Address code review issues**
  - Fix issues #6, #7, #9, #10, #11 (medium priority)
  - Address others as time permits

- [ ] **Step 7: Update README**
  - Add badges (npm, tests, type-check)
  - Add "Development" section
  - Add troubleshooting FAQ

- [ ] **Step 8: Create CONTRIBUTING.md**
  - Development setup
  - How to run tests
  - Commit message convention (Conventional Commits)
  - PR process

- [ ] **Step 9: Final verification**
  - Run all tests locally
  - Verify CI passes
  - Check npm package with `npm pack --dry-run`
  - Verify `release-please` config

---

## Verification

### Local Testing

```bash
# Run all tests
npm test

# Run with coverage
npm run test:coverage

# Run specific test file
npx vitest run src/helpers.test.ts

# Type check
npm run test:compile

# Lint
npm run test:lint

# Format check
npm run test:format

# Config validation
npm run test:config
```

### CI Verification

- Push a test commit and verify CI passes
- Check that release-please creates PR with correct version bump

### npm Package Verification

```bash
# Preview package contents
npm pack --dry-run

# Publish to npm (after review)
npm publish --access public --dry-run
```

---

## Questions for User

1. **Test coverage threshold** — What coverage % is acceptable? (typically 70-80% for projects like this)

2. **Mock strategy** — Should we mock external APIs (Unreal Speech, OpenRouter) or use real test keys?
   - Option A: Mock everything (faster, no API costs)
   - Option B: Use test API keys with rate limiting
   - Option C: Both (unit tests mock, integration tests use real keys)

3. **Release scope** — Should we include addressing ALL code review issues, or focus on just tests + CI + docs? (Some issues are low priority)

4. **Documentation hosting** — Should we generate API docs with TypeDoc and publish to GitHub Pages?

Now let me fix the config tests. The issue is that Zod 4 doesn't cascade defaults into empty nested objects. The scripts/test-config.mjs already documents this quirk. I need to adjust the tests to account for this.

Looking at the error, it says `safeParse({})` fails because the nested schemas don't get their defaults applied when the parent is an empty object.

The fix is to either:
1. Use `.extend()` or restructure the schema
2. Or test with values that actually work

Since the original test-config.mjs already acknowledges this is a Zod 4 quirk and the loadConfig() function falls back to DEFAULT_CONFIG when validation fails, I should adjust my tests to reflect the actual behavior.

content.thinking: The tests are still failing because Zod 4 doesn't cascade defaults. I need to look at the actual schema to understand how it handles defaults. Let me look at the schema.ts again and understand what Zod 4 actually does.

content.text: Let me check the actual schema behavior:

content.thinking:
Now I understand. When the nested object is empty `{}`, Zod 4 doesn't fill in defaults. But if you provide at least one field in the nested object, it applies defaults for the rest.

So:
- `{ tts: { speed: -1 } }` → success, with all defaults applied
- `{}` → success, but tts is `{}` (no defaults cascaded)

The issue is that my tests are providing partial nested objects, which should work. Let me check why they're failing...

Oh wait, I see the issue. The tests are importing from the actual schema.ts which might be structured differently. Let me look at the actual schema.ts.

I see! The actual schema.ts doesn't use `.optional().default({})` - the sub-schemas are required. So when I pass `{ tts: { voiceId: "Scarlett" } }`, the schema expects ALL the nested fields, not just one.

But wait, the Zod defaults should fill in the missing fields. Let me test this.

Ah, I see the problem now! The actual schema doesn't have `.optional().default({})` for the nested objects. This means ALL nested objects are required. When the user provides a partial config like `{ tts: { voiceId: "Scarlett" } }`, Zod expects ALL the other nested objects too (`behavior`, `summarizer`, `debug`, `api`).

But wait, looking at the actual `loadConfig()` function in index.ts, it does:
```ts
if (existsSync(CONFIG_PATH)) {
  rawConfig = JSON.parse(readFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, "utf-8"));
}
```

If the file doesn't exist, it returns `{ ...DEFAULT_CONFIG }`. If the file exists but is empty `{}`, it will fail validation.

So the tests need to provide ALL fields (or use the DEFAULT_CONFIG). But actually, looking at how loadConfig works in index.ts, it seems like it expects a full config file. Let me check if the tests should instead test with the actual loadConfig function which merges with defaults.

Actually, I think the schema is designed to validate a complete config file, and loadConfig() handles the case where there's no config file by returning DEFAULT_CONFIG. The tests should test the schema as designed (complete configs only).

Let me update the tests to test complete valid configs.

content.thinking:
The issue is that the schema doesn't allow partial objects - you need to provide all properties of the nested objects. The `fullConfig` helper spreads into nested objects, but the TypeScript type requires all properties.

I need to change the approach - instead of relying on partial types, I should create a type for the override shapes, or simply cast where needed. The simplest fix is to cast the partial objects as `never` or use type assertions.

Actually, the cleanest solution is to test the schema behavior using `safeParse` which returns `unknown` on failure or the validated data on success. For tests that want to test partial inputs, I should either:
1. Use `as unknown as SpeakConfig` for invalid inputs that should fail
2. Use the full config for valid inputs

arguments.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Run linter
        run: npm run test:lint

      - name: Run formatter check
        run: npm run test:format

      - name: Run type check
        run: npm run test:compile

      - name: Run config validation
        run: npm run test:config

  coverage:
    name: Coverage
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Generate coverage report
        run: npm run test:coverage

      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: ./coverage/lcov.info
          fail_ci_if_error: false

# pi-speak

Voice readback extension for [Pi](https://github.com/mariozechner/pi-coding-agent).

## Features

- **Voice readback** — Hear agent responses via Unreal Speech TTS
- **Voice ping** — Spoken summary when agent finishes (LLM-powered via OpenRouter, or fallback)
- **Audio caching** — Responses cached for instant replay
- **Replay shortcut** — `alt+r` to replay last response or stop playback
- **Status widget** — Shows current playback state in the UI
- **Lint feedback** — Auto-runs ESLint + Prettier after write/edit operations
- **macOS support** — Platform abstraction exists for future Linux/Windows support

## Installation

> [!NOTE]
> Not published on npm yet

1. Install the extension:

```bash
pi install git:github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak
```

2. Add your API key to your environment by sourcing `~/.env` in your shell:

```sh
# Add to your shell profile (~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, etc.)
source ~/.env

# Or set it inline:
UNREAL_SPEECH_API_KEY=your-key-here pi
```

3. Reload Pi — the extension is auto-discovered.

## Configuration

On first run, a default config is created at `~/.config/pi-speak/config.json`:

See the default config [here](./src/config/schema.json).

| Key                           | Type         | Default                   | Description                                                 |
| ----------------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `tts.voiceId`                 | string       | `"Sierra"`                | [Unreal Speech voice ID](https://docs.v8.unrealspeech.com/) |
| `tts.bitrate`                 | string       | `"192k"`                  | Audio bitrate                                               |
| `tts.speed`                   | number       | `0`                       | Speech speed (-1.0 to 1.0)                                  |
| `tts.pitch`                   | number       | `1.0`                     | Speech pitch (0.5 to 1.5)                                   |
| `tts.maxChunkChars`           | number       | `900`                     | Max chars per TTS request (API limit: 1000)                 |
| `behavior.shortcut`           | string       | `"alt+r"`                 | Keyboard shortcut for replay/stop                           |
| `behavior.pingEnabled`        | boolean      | `true`                    | Speak ping on agent_end                                     |
| `behavior.pingOnStartEnabled` | boolean      | `false`                   | Speak welcome on session_start                              |
| `behavior.fallbackPingText`   | string       | `"Work finished."`        | Fallback ping text when summarizer disabled                 |
| `summarizer.enabled`          | boolean      | `true`                    | Use LLM for ping summaries (vs fallback)                    |
| `summarizer.model`            | string       | `"openai/gpt-oss-20b"`    | [OpenRouter model](https://openrouter.ai/models)            |
| `summarizer.maxTokens`        | number       | `60`                      | Max tokens for summarizer                                   |
| `summarizer.timeoutMs`        | number       | `5000`                    | Timeout for summarizer API call                             |
| `debug.enabled`               | boolean      | `true`                    | Write to debug log                                          |
| `debug.logPath`               | string       | `"~/.pi-speak-debug.log"` | Path to debug log file                                      |
| `debug.logMaxBytes`           | number       | `2097152`                 | Max size before log rotation                                |
| `api.unrealSpeechKey`         | string\|null | `null`                    | Unreal Speech API key (env var takes precedence)            |
| `api.openRouterKey`           | string\|null | `null`                    | OpenRouter API key (env var takes precedence)               |

## Usage

- **Notification**: When the agent finishes, a notification appears with the session name and summary. Navigate to the session yourself, then press `alt+r` to hear the response.
- **`alt+r`**: Press to replay the last response. Press again during playback to stop.

## Debugging

Set `PI_SPEAK_DEBUG=0` to disable the debug log, or set `"debug": false` in config.
Log file: `~/.pi-speak-debug.log`

## Releases

This project uses [release-please](https://github.com/googleapis/release-please) for automated versioning and changelog generation via [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).

## TODO

- [x] Add release-please
- [x] Config location follows XDG spec (`~/.config/pi-speak/config.json`) — no standard convention exists for pi-package extension config
- [x] Config Migration Support
- [x] Remove ~/.env file loading (use process.env directly)
- [x] If muted, don't generate TTS to save costs.
  - don't play the summarizer, and show notification for the readback inside the TUI that its muted
- [ ] Publish on npm
- [ ] Linux support
- [ ] Windows support

# pi-speak

[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@nbr/pi-speak.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@nbr/pi-speak)
[![Test Status](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/actions)
[![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.0-blue)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

Voice readback extension for [Pi](https://github.com/mariozechner/pi-coding-agent).

## Features

- **Voice readback** — Hear agent responses via Unreal Speech TTS
- **Voice ping** — Spoken summary when agent finishes (LLM-powered via OpenRouter, or fallback)
- **Audio caching** — Responses cached for instant replay
- **Replay shortcut** — `alt+r` to replay last response or stop playback
- **Status widget** — Shows current playback state in the UI
- **Lint feedback** — Auto-runs ESLint + Prettier after write/edit operations
- **macOS support** — Platform abstraction exists for future Linux/Windows support

## Installation

1. Install the extension:

```bash
pi install npm:@nbr/pi-speak
```

2. Add your API key to your environment by sourcing `~/.env` in your shell:

```sh
# Add to your shell profile (~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, etc.)
source ~/.env

# Or set it inline:
UNREAL_SPEECH_API_KEY=your-key-here pi
```

3. Reload Pi — the extension is auto-discovered.

## Configuration

On first run, a default config is created at `~/.config/pi-speak/config.json`:

See the default config [here](./src/config/schema.json).

| Key                           | Type         | Default                   | Description                                                 |
| ----------------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `tts.voiceId`                 | string       | `"Sierra"`                | [Unreal Speech voice ID](https://docs.v8.unrealspeech.com/) |
| `tts.bitrate`                 | string       | `"192k"`                  | Audio bitrate                                               |
| `tts.speed`                   | number       | `0`                       | Speech speed (-1.0 to 1.0)                                  |
| `tts.pitch`                   | number       | `1.0`                     | Speech pitch (0.5 to 1.5)                                   |
| `tts.maxChunkChars`           | number       | `900`                     | Max chars per TTS request (API limit: 1000)                 |
| `behavior.shortcut`           | string       | `"alt+r"`                 | Keyboard shortcut for replay/stop                           |
| `behavior.pingEnabled`        | boolean      | `true`                    | Speak ping on agent_end                                     |
| `behavior.pingOnStartEnabled` | boolean      | `false`                   | Speak welcome on session_start                              |
| `behavior.fallbackPingText`   | string       | `"Work finished."`        | Fallback ping text when summarizer disabled                 |
| `summarizer.enabled`          | boolean      | `true`                    | Use LLM for ping summaries (vs fallback)                    |
| `summarizer.model`            | string       | `"openai/gpt-oss-20b"`    | [OpenRouter model](https://openrouter.ai/models)            |
| `summarizer.maxTokens`        | number       | `60`                      | Max tokens for summarizer                                   |
| `summarizer.timeoutMs`        | number       | `5000`                    | Timeout for summarizer API call                             |
| `debug.enabled`               | boolean      | `true`                    | Write to debug log                                          |
| `debug.logPath`               | string       | `"~/.pi-speak-debug.log"` | Path to debug log file                                      |
| `debug.logMaxBytes`           | number       | `2097152`                 | Max size before log rotation                                |
| `api.unrealSpeechKey`         | string\|null | `null`                    | Unreal Speech API key (env var takes precedence)            |
| `api.openRouterKey`           | string\|null | `null`                    | OpenRouter API key (env var takes precedence)               |

## Usage

- **Notification**: When the agent finishes, a notification appears with the session name and summary. Navigate to the session yourself, then press `alt+r` to hear the response.
- **`alt+r`**: Press to replay the last response. Press again during playback to stop.

## Debugging

Set `PI_SPEAK_DEBUG=0` to disable the debug log, or set `"debug": false` in config.
Log file: `~/.pi-speak-debug.log`

## Development

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+
- npm

### Setup

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak.git
cd pi-speak

# Install dependencies
npm install
```

### Testing

```bash
# Run all tests
npm test

# Run tests in watch mode
npm run test:watch

# Run tests with coverage
npm run test:coverage

# Run all quality checks
npm run test:all
```

### Quality Assurance

```bash
# Lint
npm run test:lint

# Format check
npm run test:format

# Type check
npm run test:compile

# Config validation
npm run test:config

# Fix lint and format issues
npm run lint
npm run format
```

### Project Structure

```
src/
├── index.ts           # Main extension entry point
├── tts.ts             # TTS playback and caching
├── summarizer.ts      # LLM-powered ping summarization
├── platform.ts        # Platform abstraction (macOS)
├── debug.ts           # Debug logging
├── helpers.ts         # Text processing utilities
└── config/
    ├── index.ts       # Config loading and initialization
    ├── schema.ts      # Zod schemas
    ├── defaults.ts    # Default values
    ├── migrations.ts  # Version migrations
    ├── constants.ts   # Constants
    └── schema.json    # JSON Schema for IDE autocomplete
```

## Releases

This project uses [release-please](https://github.com/googleapis/release-please) for automated versioning and changelog generation via [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).

## Troubleshooting

### Voice readback not working

1. Ensure `UNREAL_SPEECH_API_KEY` is set in your environment
2. Check if system audio is not muted
3. Look at `~/.pi-speak-debug.log` for error messages

### Extension not loading

1. Verify the extension is installed: `pi install npm:@nbr/pi-speak`
2. Check Pi logs for extension errors
3. Ensure you're on macOS (Linux/Windows audio not yet supported)

### Config issues

The config file is at `~/.config/pi-speak/config.json`. Delete it to reset to defaults.

## License

MIT

arguments.content:
# Contributing to pi-speak

Thank you for your interest in contributing to pi-speak!

## Getting Started

1. Fork the repository
2. Clone your fork: `git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/pi-speak.git`
3. Install dependencies: `npm install`
4. Create a branch: `git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name`

## Development Workflow

### Running Tests

```bash
# Run all tests
npm test

# Run tests in watch mode (for development)
npm run test:watch

# Run tests with coverage report
npm run test:coverage
```

### Quality Checks

Before submitting a PR, ensure all checks pass:

```bash
# Run all quality checks
npm run test:all

# Or run individually:
npm run test:lint      # ESLint
npm run test:format    # Prettier
npm run test:compile   # TypeScript
npm run test:config    # Config validation
```

### Fixing Issues

```bash
# Auto-fix lint issues and format
npm run lint
npm run format
```

## Commit Message Convention

This project follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):

```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer]
```

### Types

- `feat`: A new feature
- `fix`: A bug fix
- `docs`: Documentation only changes
- `refactor`: Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- `perf`: A code change that improves performance
- `test`: Adding missing tests or correcting existing tests
- `chore`: Changes to the build process or auxiliary tools

### Examples

```
feat(tts): add audio caching for instant replay
fix(summarizer): handle empty API responses gracefully
docs(readme): add troubleshooting section
refactor(config): use Zod for runtime validation
test(helpers): add tests for stripMarkdown function
```

## Code Style

- TypeScript with strict mode
- 2-space indentation
- Single quotes for strings
- Semicolons required
- ESLint + Prettier for formatting

## Pull Request Process

1. **Keep PRs focused**: Each PR should address a single concern
2. **Update tests**: Add tests for new functionality
3. **Update docs**: Keep README and code comments current
4. **Follow conventions**: Use Conventional Commits for commit messages
5. **Pass CI**: Ensure all checks pass before requesting review

## Reporting Issues

When reporting bugs, please include:

- Node.js version (`node --version`)
- macOS version
- pi-speak version (from package.json)
- Steps to reproduce
- Expected vs actual behavior
- Relevant log output from `~/.pi-speak-debug.log`

## Questions?

Feel free to open an issue for questions or discussions.

## Context

Preparing the first npm release (`@nbr/pi-speak`) for the pi-speak voice readback extension. The codebase is functional but lacks:
- **Unit tests** (no test framework)
- **CI/CD quality gates** (lint/type-check not in CI)
- **Complete documentation** for contributors
- **Code quality improvements** from previous review

---

## Approach

1. **Add test framework** (Vitest) with unit tests for pure functions
2. **Set up CI quality gates** — run lint, type-check, and tests on PR
3. **Address code review issues** — review the 14 issues found previously
4. **Improve documentation** — add CONTRIBUTING.md, improve README
5. **Verify release setup** — ensure npm metadata is complete

---

## Reuse

| `chunkBySentences(text, maxChars)` | `src/helpers.ts:46` | Split text into chunks |
| `summarize(text, maxLen)` | `src/helpers.ts:85` | One-line summary |

content.text:
---
name: dev-util-commit
description: Create a git commit with conventional commit format, scope detection, and clean history strategies. Load when committing code changes.
user-invocable: true
argument-hint: [optional commit message override]
---

# Commit

Create a commit following conventional commit conventions.

## Format

```
<type>(<scope>): <description>
```

### Types

`feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `chore`, `docs`, `perf`, `ci`, `test`

### Scope

Determined by which files changed. Project-specific scope rules override these defaults:

- If changes are isolated to one module/directory, use that as scope
- If changes span multiple areas, omit scope
- Config-only or docs-only changes: omit scope

### Description

- 1 sentence, imperative mood ("add X" not "added X")
- Focus on the _why_, not the _what_
- Keep under 70 characters

## Rules

- **Every commit must be green** — tests pass, lint clean.
- Each commit should represent a distinct, working change.
- Stage specific files (`git add <file>`) — avoid `git add -A` which can catch secrets or unrelated changes.
- Always use heredoc for commit messages to preserve formatting.

## Strategies

Choose based on what you're fixing:

| Situation                                 | Strategy           | Command                        |
| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------ |
| Fix belongs to the **previous** commit    | Amend              | `git commit --amend --no-edit` |
| Fix belongs to an **older** commit        | Fixup + autosquash | See below                      |
| Change is a **new distinct unit** of work | New commit         | Normal commit                  |

**Always ask before amending or fixup.** These rewrite history and can go wrong.

### Fixup workflow

```bash
# 1. Find the target commit
git log --oneline -10

# 2. Create a fixup commit pointing to the target
git commit --fixup=<sha>

# 3. Autosquash to merge the fixup into the target commit
git rebase -i --autosquash <sha>~1
```

## Process

1. Check `git status` and `git diff --staged` to understand what's being committed
2. Determine scope from changed file paths
3. Decide strategy: amend, fixup, or new commit
4. Draft a concise message focusing on the _why_
5. Stage specific files
6. Commit via heredoc for proper formatting

## 🤖 I have created a release _beep_ _boop_

## [0.2.0](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/compare/pi-speak-v0.1.0...pi-speak-v0.2.0) (2026-04-10)

### Features

*   add lint extension for pi coding agent ([fe617f6](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/fe617f6030bd09ed8f0d29c1d8ed9aed33d0935b))
*   add test infrastructure and CI for npm release ([0eae8d6](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/0eae8d659e607fbd2667f4bd20388391456268f8))
*   **lint:** add compile check to post-execution validation ([7e4d701](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/7e4d701055e74c5442597c9508f91b71dccafb4d))
*   publish pi-speak package to npm on release ([9fe9bec](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/9fe9bec7426c0ec413888f642238dede9f19de19))
*   skip TTS generation when system is muted to save costs ([5804aae](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/5804aaead9fa9004a8015e92290c1f87aa27d9b0))

### Refactors

*   **config:** remove env var override support ([d2bbad3](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/d2bbad31f78ef81689f108e2793fcfe756db182c))
*   **config:** restructure config into modular folder with Zod validation ([8a44174](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/8a44174178166d2c212e9c45120b8ee65cfd10eb))
*   move imports to top of config.ts ([ea7e0f5](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/ea7e0f5b26881b5e1eefb395339c8cb3ea5584ec))
*   remove ~/.env file loading, use process.env directly ([f87551d](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/f87551dfa4efd2a69a43ff18387103e9ad414f85))
*   remove unused shellQuote function and dead imports ([1d57b6d](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/1d57b6dc08f19b3a48e6afda6a328438eb21ef44))
*   rename lint extension to checks ([45c81e4](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/45c81e4f7af58fd5ed870d3b991280403524b11f))
*   share UI interface type instead of inline literals ([7d31352](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/7d31352ed5d76e62c516f584a5f53c2277d4359f))

### Bug Fixes

*   add debug logging and handle edge case for muted regeneration ([5804aae](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/5804aaead9fa9004a8015e92290c1f87aa27d9b0))
*   add platform detection with user warning for unsupported OS ([c89a257](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/c89a257e7e6a63c74a17fbcccd41b1060257fb9b))
*   add timeout to summarizer fetch call ([39c8a5a](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/39c8a5a646268d3bcaf5d84e65c9b8fc1952d8df))
*   add timeout to TTS fetch call ([845231c](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/845231c3be40e765798208a0196087b658f0cb54))
*   clean up orphaned cache files on playback abort ([04cfda6](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/04cfda6041dbe0f93466ae2333b5f04dcd332fce))
*   improve error handling in debug logging with console fallback ([240e543](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/240e543fc2c7ff06a27264f70a1ca45dd17b0fe4))
*   **lint:** use test:format script for formatting checks ([052a024](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/052a02401ae59a00beee79482a339419897cbb63))
*   move pi-coding-agent to peerDependencies ([e15e844](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/e15e84446838d19514756be0195b1d0612f737ec))
*   persist migrated config to file and update README with nested structure ([29ab3ec](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/29ab3ec8716942bfb5fb271bbfef11908a619ff5))
*   **tts:** set UNREAL\_SPEECH\_API\_KEY in tests for CI ([d6ec5d9](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/d6ec5d91dcb1fe1322e9dd2f03a000711497309c))
*   use SIGKILL fallback in stop() for reliable playback termination ([d3e3cf6](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/d3e3cf684f466d6e93c858e4cdf6c59e31a3f4a3))
*   wire config.debug into debug module ([a0ec315](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/a0ec31549ac37411cce690d8ccf5a489ee955add))

### Documentation

*   add summarizerModel config option and update table ([db08c75](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/db08c750b0912ff7f5026668591a06a7e1bc1841))
*   document speak() is not re-entrant ([af9cd29](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/af9cd29eace020056716fc2b9f9b1ffea92a4756))
*   replace config example with link to schema.json ([5773c5b](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/5773c5b1eda51e7962d489e0000dd4efbb4ac8e8))
*   sync README with code changes and update TODO ([4611ae5](https://github.com/nikbrunner/pi-speak/commit/4611ae5f9b774229dc1357231221cc250d772bb5))

The PR shows it's releasing 0.2.0 (not 1.0.0 as I mentioned earlier). That makes sense - it goes from 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 because:

1. The current version is 0.1.0
2. The commits since then include features and fixes, so it's a minor bump (0.1.x → 0.2.0)

Release-please uses semver:
- Patch (0.1.0 → 0.1.1): Bug fixes
- Minor (0.1.0 → 0.2.0): Features + fixes
- Major (0.1.0 → 1.0.0): Breaking changes

Since there are new features, it bumps the minor version. This is correct behavior.

content.text:
You're right, I misremembered. The PR shows **0.2.0** — which is correct! 

Release-please bumps:
- `0.1.0` → `0.1.1` for patches only
- `0.1.0` → `0.2.0` for features (which you have)
- Would be `1.0.0` for breaking changes

Since you added tests, CI, and new features, the minor bump to 0.2.0 is right. Merging this PR will publish to npm.

# Setup Pre-Commit Hooks

Uses native git `core.hooksPath` — no Husky or wrapper deps.

## Step 0: Detect Existing Setup

Before doing anything, check if the project already has pre-commit hooks configured.

**Look for:**

| Signal                               | Tool                |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------- |
| `.husky/` directory                  | Husky               |
| `lefthook.yml` or `.lefthook.yml`    | Lefthook            |
| `.pre-commit-config.yaml`            | pre-commit (Python) |
| `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (non-sample) | Manual git hooks    |
| `"husky"` in package.json deps       | Husky               |
| `"prepare": "husky"` in scripts      | Husky               |
| `"lint-staged"` in package.json      | lint-staged (keep)  |

**If an existing setup is found:**

1. **Inventory** what the current hooks do — list every command/check that runs
2. **Present findings** to the user: what tool is in use, what checks it runs, what config files exist
3. **Propose migration** — map each existing check to the `.githooks/` equivalent, flag anything that would be lost or changed
4. **Wait for user approval** before making changes
5. **Clean up** the old tool: remove config files, uninstall deps, remove `prepare` scripts that reference the old tool

**If no existing setup is found:** proceed to Step 1.

## Step 1: Detect Ecosystem

| Signal                      | Ecosystem                 |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| `deno.json` or `deno.jsonc` | Deno                      |
| `package.json` + lockfile   | Node                      |
| Both present                | Ask user which is primary |

## Step 2: Configure Hooks

### Deno Projects

No extra dependencies needed. Deno has everything built in.

**Create `.githooks/pre-commit`:**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env sh
set -e

deno fmt --check
deno lint
deno check .
deno test
```

**Adapt**: If the project has no tests yet, omit `deno test` and tell the user. If `deno.json` has a `test` task, use `deno task test` instead.

**Add `install-hooks` task to deno.json:**

```json
{
  "tasks": {
    "install-hooks": "git config core.hooksPath .githooks && echo 'Hooks installed.'"
  }
}
```

> Deno has no `prepare` lifecycle hook like Node's `npm install`. An explicit task is the Deno equivalent — collaborators run `deno task install-hooks` after cloning.

### Node Projects

**Detect package manager**: Check for `package-lock.json` (npm), `pnpm-lock.yaml` (pnpm), `yarn.lock` (yarn), `bun.lockb` (bun). Default to npm if unclear.

**Install devDependencies:**

```
lint-staged prettier
```

**Create `.githooks/pre-commit`:**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env sh
set -e

npx lint-staged
npm run typecheck
npm run test
```

**Adapt**: Replace `npm`/`npx` with detected package manager. If repo has no `typecheck` or `test` script in package.json, omit those lines and tell the user.

**Create `.lintstagedrc`:**

```json
{
  "*": "prettier --ignore-unknown --check"
}
```

> **Why `--check` instead of `--write`**: Using `--write` in a pre-commit hook silently reformats files, but the changes land in the working tree — not in the staged area. This causes phantom diffs after every commit. `--check` fails the commit instead, so the developer runs `prettier --write .` (or an npm script) explicitly, stages the result, and commits clean.

**Create `.prettierrc` (only if no Prettier config exists):**

```json
{
  "useTabs": false,
  "tabWidth": 2,
  "printWidth": 80,
  "singleQuote": false,
  "trailingComma": "es5",
  "semi": true,
  "arrowParens": "always"
}
```

**Add `prepare` script to package.json:**

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "prepare": "git config core.hooksPath .githooks"
  }
}
```

## Step 3: Enable Hooks

```bash
chmod +x .githooks/pre-commit
git config core.hooksPath .githooks
```

## Step 4: Verify

- [ ] `.githooks/pre-commit` exists and is executable
- [ ] `git config core.hooksPath` returns `.githooks`
- [ ] **Deno only**: `install-hooks` task exists in `deno.json`
- [ ] **Node only**: `.lintstagedrc` exists, Prettier config exists, `prepare` script set
- [ ] Run a dry-run of the hook: `./.githooks/pre-commit`

## Step 5: Commit

Stage all changed/created files and commit: `chore: add pre-commit hooks`

This runs through the new hooks — a good smoke test.

## Formatting Strategy: Check, Don't Write

Both ecosystems use **`--check` mode** in the hook — the hook **rejects** unformatted code rather than silently rewriting it.

Why: `--write` in a pre-commit hook modifies files in the working tree but not the staging area. This causes ghost diffs after every commit. With `--check`, the developer formats explicitly, stages the result, and commits clean.

**Convenience scripts to add to package.json / deno.json:**

- Node: `"format": "prettier --ignore-unknown --write ."` → run before committing
- Deno: `deno fmt` → already available, no config needed

## Notes

- `core.hooksPath` is git-native (git 2.9+), no wrapper needed
- Deno projects need zero extra deps — `deno fmt`/`lint`/`check`/`test` cover everything
- Node's `prepare` script ensures hooks are configured on `npm install` for collaborators
- `prettier --ignore-unknown` skips files Prettier can't parse (images, etc.)
- For non-JS/TS repos, skip lint-staged and write shell commands directly in the hook
</skill>

content.thinking:
Wait, I just wrote the same thing. The issue is that prettier runs on all files, but test:format only runs on src/. But test:all runs test:format which runs prettier on src/ only. So prettier --check . runs on all files, which is actually a superset of what test:format does.

So the current hook is fine - it runs prettier on everything, then runs test:all which includes prettier on src/. It's slightly redundant but not incorrect.

But actually the user might prefer to just have npm run test:all since it already includes everything including the prettier check on src. The difference is:
- Current: prettier on all files + test:all (which includes prettier on src)
- Cleaner: just test:all

Let me check what test:all actually does again and simplify.

content.text:
Actually, since `test:all` already includes `test:format`, I could simplify to just:

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh

npm run test:all
```

But `test:format` only checks `src/`, not workflow files or scripts. Your choice:
- **Current**: Full prettier check on all files + full test suite
- **Simpler**: Just test suite (which includes src/ format check)

Which do you prefer?

content.thinking:
I see - the user changed:
- `prettier --check src` → `prettier --check .`
- `prettier --write src` → `prettier --write --ignore-unknown .`

The problem is that `prettier --check .` now scans ALL files in the project, including `.githooks/pre-commit`. Prettier finds `prettier.config.ts` and tries to parse it as TypeScript, then also tries to parse `.githooks/pre-commit` which has a `.ts` extension issue... wait, no, the error says it can't parse prettier.config.ts as TypeScript.

Actually, looking at the error more carefully:
```
[error] Unknown file extension ".ts" for /home/runner/work/pi-speak/pi-speak/prettier.config.ts
```

This means prettier.config.ts can't be parsed. In CI, they don't have jiti or TypeScript support set up for Prettier. Prettier by default uses the TypeScript parser for `.ts` files, but it needs the right setup.

Actually, Prettier 3.x should handle TypeScript natively without jiti if it's installed. But the issue is that prettier.config.ts is being loaded as a config file AND then being checked as a file.

Wait, looking at the error again:
```
[error] Invalid configuration for file "/home/runner/work/pi-speak/pi-speak/.githooks/pre-commit":
[error] Unknown file extension ".ts" for /home/runner/work/pi-speak/pi-speak/prettier.config.ts
```

This is weird - it says `.githooks/pre-commit` has an invalid configuration because of prettier.config.ts? That doesn't make sense...

Oh wait, I think I misread the error. Let me look again:

```
[error] Invalid configuration for file "/home/runner/work/pi-speak/pi-speak/.githooks/pre-commit":


```

The error is about `.githooks/pre-commit` not `.githooks/pre-commit` having a TypeScript extension issue. The second error says prettier.config.ts can't be parsed with TypeScript. These look like two separate issues.

When running `prettier --check .` in CI, Prettier encounters `.githooks/pre-commit` as a file without an extension and tries to use prettier.config.ts as the configuration. But prettier.config.ts can't be parsed because the TypeScript parser isn't properly configured in the CI environment, even though it's installed locally with jiti support.

```
[error] Unknown file extension ".ts" for /home/runner/work/pi-speak/pi-speak/prettier.config.ts
```

The error is about the config file itself failing to load, not about checking the file. The issue is that Prettier can't parse the `.ts` config file, which is a different problem from what I initially thought.

queries.query: ["prettier Unknown file extension .ts config file", "prettier 3.x typescript config parsing error"]
queries.answer:
The issue with "Unknown file extension .ts config file" when using Prettier 3.x, especially with TypeScript configuration files like `prettier.config.ts`, stems from recent changes in support for TypeScript config files. According to a pull request merged in February 2024 ([Prettier #16112](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/16112)), support for TypeScript config files (`prettier.config.ts`, `.prettierrc.ts`, etc.) was added, but this feature was not fully supported in all tools or extensions immediately afterward.

In particular, the Prettier VS Code extension had a bug where it did not support `prettier.config.ts` files after the feature was introduced in version 3.5.0 ([Prettier VSCode Issue #3623](https://github.com/prettier/prettier-vscode/issues/3623), published on 2025-02-10). The error logs show that node's ESM loader does not recognize the `.ts` extension, leading to the "Unknown file extension" error. Additionally, other issues like Windows path handling and configuration resolution bugs have been reported ([Prettier VSCode #3062](https://github.com/prettier/prettier-vscode/issues/3062), [Prettier #15841](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/15841)).

While support was added in Prettier core, extensions and tooling like VS Code may require updates to fully handle `.ts` config files without errors. As of early 2026, users encountering this error should ensure they are using the latest versions of Prettier and its extensions, and consider downgrading to earlier versions (e.g., v2.8.8) if support is still incomplete ([Workaround in #3062](https://github.com/prettier/prettier-vscode/issues/3062)). Also, note that TypeScript config support requires specific setup, such as Node.js 22.6.0+ and `--experimental-strip-types` flags, which can influence parsing ([Prettier documentation](https://prettier.io/docs/next/configuration)).

In summary, the "Unknown file extension .ts" error with Prettier 3.x and TypeScript config files is due to incomplete support in tooling, and keeping tools updated or using compatible Node.js versions may resolve the issue.

content.text:
[These results were manually curated by the user in the browser. Use them as-is — do not re-search or discard.]

The issue with "Unknown file extension .ts config file" when using Prettier 3.x, especially with TypeScript configuration files like `prettier.config.ts`, stems from recent changes in support for TypeScript config files. According to a pull request merged in February 2024 ([Prettier #16112](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/16112)), support for TypeScript config files (`prettier.config.ts`, `.prettierrc.ts`, etc.) was added, but this feature was not fully supported in all tools or extensions immediately afterward.

In particular, the Prettier VS Code extension had a bug where it did not support `prettier.config.ts` files after the feature was introduced in version 3.5.0 ([Prettier VSCode Issue #3623](https://github.com/prettier/prettier-vscode/issues/3623), published on 2025-02-10). The error logs show that node's ESM loader does not recognize the `.ts` extension, leading to the "Unknown file extension" error. Additionally, other issues like Windows path handling and configuration resolution bugs have been reported ([Prettier VSCode #3062](https://github.com/prettier/prettier-vscode/issues/3062), [Prettier #15841](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/15841)).

While support was added in Prettier core, extensions and tooling like VS Code may require updates to fully handle `.ts` config files without errors. As of early 2026, users encountering this error should ensure they are using the latest versions of Prettier and its extensions, and consider downgrading to earlier versions (e.g., v2.8.8) if support is still incomplete ([Workaround in #3062](https://github.com/prettier/prettier-vscode/issues/3062)). Also, note that TypeScript config support requires specific setup, such as Node.js 22.6.0+ and `--experimental-strip-types` flags, which can influence parsing ([Prettier documentation](https://prettier.io/docs/next/configuration)).

In summary, the "Unknown file extension .ts" error with Prettier 3.x and TypeScript config files is due to incomplete support in tooling, and keeping tools updated or using compatible Node.js versions may resolve the issue.

---

name: release-please
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  release-please:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      releases_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs.releases_created }}
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        id: release
        with:
          config-file: .github/release-please-config.json
          manifest-file: .github/.release-please-manifest.json

  publish:
    needs: release-please
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only publish when a new release was created
    if: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.releases_created == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Publish to npm
        run: npm publish --access public
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Get Node version from package.json
        id: node-version
        run: echo "version=$(cat package.json | jq -r '.engines.node' | sed 's/>=//')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ steps.node-version.outputs.version }}
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Run linter
        run: npm run test:lint

      - name: Run formatter check
        run: npm run test:format

      - name: Run type check
        run: npm run test:compile

      - name: Run config validation

on:

jobs:
  release-please:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:

    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        id: release
        with:
          config-file: .github/release-please-config.json
          manifest-file: .github/.release-please-manifest.json

  publish:
    needs: release-please
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only publish when a new release was created
    if: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.releases_created == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:

          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
